quizQuestion:
Si {___} más despacio, lo entendería.
(If he spoke more slowly, I would understand.)
sentenceTranslation: If he spoke more slowly, I would understand.
explanation: hablase — imperfect subjunctive -se form of hablar (él/ella). The -se form is the literary alternative to the -ra form (hablara). Both are equally correct. Pattern: hablase, hablases, hablase, hablásemos, hablaseis, hablasen.

quizQuestion:
Esperaba que {___} a tiempo.
(He hoped they would arrive on time.)
sentenceTranslation: He hoped they would arrive on time.
explanation: llegasen — imperfect subjunctive -se form of llegar (ellos/ellas). Ellos/ellas form of the -se paradigm: -asen. Both llegasen and llegaran are correct.

quizQuestion:
Era necesario que {___} antes de venir.
(It was necessary for you (pl.) to call before coming.)
sentenceTranslation: It was necessary for you (pl.) to call before coming.
explanation: llamaseis — imperfect subjunctive -se form of llamar (vosotros). The vosotros form ends in -aseis. This is the literary -se alternative to the -ra form llamarais.

quizQuestion:
Era necesario que {___} más en equipo.
(It was necessary that we work more as a team.)
sentenceTranslation: It was necessary that we work more as a team.
explanation: trabajásemos — imperfect subjunctive -se form of trabajar (nosotros). Note the written accent on the á. The nosotros -se form always has a written accent: trabajásemos.

quizQuestion:
El médico le dijo que {___} menos para estar más sano.
(The doctor told him to eat less to be healthier.)
sentenceTranslation: The doctor told him to eat less to be healthier.
explanation: comiese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of comer (él/ella). After le dijo que (reported speech / past indirect request), use imperfect subjunctive. -se paradigm: comiese, comieses, comiese, comiésemos, comieseis, comiesen.

quizQuestion:
Quería que {___} más cerca.
(She wanted him to live closer.)
sentenceTranslation: She wanted him to live closer.
explanation: viviese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of vivir (él/ella). Pattern: viviese, vivieses, viviese, viviésemos, vivieseis, viviesen. Interchangeable with the -ra form viviera.

quizQuestion:
Era importante que {___} pronto.
(It was important that they write soon.)
sentenceTranslation: It was important that they write soon.
explanation: escribiesen — imperfect subjunctive -se form of escribir (ellos/ellas). The ellos/ellas -se form ends in -iesen for -ir verbs: escribiesen.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} posible, lo haría encantado.
(If it were possible, I would gladly do it.)
sentenceTranslation: If it were possible, I would gladly do it.
explanation: fuese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of ser (él/ella). Ser and ir share the same imperfect subjunctive forms. -se paradigm: fuese, fueses, fuese, fuésemos, fueseis, fuesen. Interchangeable with fuera.

quizQuestion:
Ojalá {___} más suerte.
(I wish I had more luck.)
sentenceTranslation: I wish I had more luck.
explanation: tuviese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of tener (yo). Derived from preterite stem tuv-. -se paradigm: tuviese, tuvieses, tuviese, tuviésemos, tuvieseis, tuviesen.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} venir, vendría encantado.
(If I could come, I would gladly come.)
sentenceTranslation: If I could come, I would gladly come.
explanation: pudiese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of poder (yo). Derived from preterite stem pud-. -se form: pudiese. Interchangeable with pudiera.

quizQuestion:
Quería que {___} la verdad.
(She wanted him to tell the truth.)
sentenceTranslation: She wanted him to tell the truth.
explanation: dijese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of decir (él/ella). Derived from preterite dij- stem. -se form: dijese. Pattern: dijese, dijeses, dijese, dijésemos, dijeseis, dijesen.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} aquí, sería mejor.
(If he were here, it would be better.)
sentenceTranslation: If he were here, it would be better.
explanation: estuviese — imperfect subjunctive -se form of estar (él/ella). Stem: estuv-. -se form: estuviese. Interchangeable with estuviera in all contexts.
